habitat: Forests in mountain areas; bushes; bamboo woods; secondary forest; parks; and gardens
story: In the diverse world of birds, the Chinese Hwamei (Garrulax canorus) stands out. This species, part of the Leiothrichidae family, is a resident of Forests in mountain areas; bushes; bamboo woods; secondary forest; parks; and gardens. Its IUCN Red List status is Least Concern (LC), which serves as a call to action for habitat preservation. The Chinese Hwamei is more than just a bird; it is a symbol of the rich biodiversity that we must protect. Through this poster, we hope to foster a greater understanding and appreciation for the Chinese Hwamei and its role in the ecosystem.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
